schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

schemas.name: Income Tax Notice Reply — Sections 142-148

step.text: Read notice carefully — identify section and response date

mainEntity.name: I received a Section 143(1) intimation. Is this a notice?

acceptedAnswer.text: Section 143(1) is an automated processing intimation, not a notice. It shows the tax department computed result vs. your filed return. If there is a demand, you can either accept (pay) or file a rectification request (Section 154) if you disagree.

A reassessment notice — the AO believes income has escaped assessment. You must file a return in response. We evaluate the validity of the notice (was proper procedure followed?) and draft the response/return accordingly.

mainEntity.name: How quickly must I respond to an IT notice?

acceptedAnswer.text: Varies by notice type. Section 142(1): typically 15-30 days. Section 143(2): as specified (usually 15-21 days). Section 148: 30 days to file return. Always respond within the deadline — non-response can lead to ex-parte assessment.

mainEntity.name: What types of IT notices can I receive?

acceptedAnswer.text: Section 142(1): information/return filing. Section 143(2): scrutiny. Section 148/148A: reassessment. Section 156: demand. Section 245: set-off of refund. Each has different response timeline and implications.

mainEntity.name: What is the time limit to respond to IT notice?

acceptedAnswer.text: Section 142(1): 15 days (extendable). Section 143(2): 30 days. Section 148A: 7-30 days. Non-response: assessment completed on best judgment basis, penalties imposed. Always respond within time even if requesting extension.

mainEntity.name: Can I respond to IT notices myself?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can, but not recommended for 143(2) scrutiny or 148 reassessment. AO questions require strategic responses — admitting one point may create liability on another. Professional representation protects against self-incriminating responses.

Since 2020, most IT assessments are faceless (no physical meetings). National Faceless Assessment Centre issues notices and orders electronically. Response filed on e-portal. Video conferencing for hearings. Reduces corruption but requires precise written responses.

mainEntity.name: What happens if I ignore an IT notice?

acceptedAnswer.text: Section 144: best judgment assessment (AO estimates income at high levels). Section 271(1)(b): penalty of ₹10,000 for non-compliance. Prosecution possible under Section 276D. Tax demand created without your input. Never ignore.
